item.title: About
item.description:
Introduction

The Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) and its generalizations in the form of Tensor Network States have made significant contributions across a wide range of fields, including condensed matter physics, statistical physics, quantum chemistry, nuclear physics, quantum information, and machine learning.

The Tensor Network States: Algorithms and Applications (TNSAA) workshops were established to highlight these developments, providing a platform for presenting new algorithmic advances and their applications in uncovering novel physics.

item.title: Call for Papers
item.description:
TNSAA 2026 is an international workshop on tensor network states, algorithms, and applications.

In addition to invited talks, the workshop welcomes contributed presentations from researchers and students working in related areas.

Topics

Topics include, but are not limited to:

  • Tensor network algorithms
  • Quantum many-body systems
  • Quantum information and computation
  • Numerical methods for strongly correlated systems
  • Machine learning and tensor networks
  • Quantum simulation
  • Applications of tensor networks in physics, chemistry, and related fields

Presentation Formats

Contributed presentations may be accepted as:

item.title: Contact
item.description:
For inquiries regarding TNSAA 2026, please contact:

TNSAA 2026 Organizing Committee

Email:
ueda.hiroshi.qiqb@osaka-u.ac.jp

Venue

RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS)
Kobe, Japan

item.title: News
item.description:
Registration is now open

Registration for TNSAA 2026 is now open.

The registration deadline is November 15, 2026.

Participants who plan to attend the banquet / networking reception are also asked to complete the banquet fee payment by November 15, 2026.

Please visit the Registration page for details.

Website launched

The official website of TNSAA 2026 is now online.

Venue confirmed

TNSAA 2026 will be held at the RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS), Kobe, Japan.

item.title: Program
item.description:
The detailed program will be announced closer to the workshop dates.

Tentative Schedule

Date Program
December 15 (Tue) Registration / Opening / Invited Talks / Contributed Talks
December 16 (Wed) Invited Talks / Contributed Talks / Poster Session
December 17 (Thu) Invited Talks / Contributed Talks
December 18 (Fri) Invited Talks / Contributed Talks / Closing

Invited Talks

Invited talks will be scheduled throughout the workshop.

Contributed Presentations

TNSAA 2026 will include contributed oral and poster presentations.

item.title: Registration
item.description:
Registration for TNSAA 2026 is now open.

Please complete the primary registration form below.
If you are unable to access Google Forms from your region or institution, please use the alternative registration form.

Primary Registration Form Alternative Registration Form

Please submit only one registration form. Do not submit both forms.

Some parts of the alternative form interface may appear in Chinese, but responses may be submitted in English.

item.title: Speakers
item.description:
Invited speakers will be announced soon.

Invited Speakers

TBA

To be announced

Invited speakers will be listed here.

Contributed Presentations

TNSAA 2026 will welcome contributed oral and poster presentations.

Submission details will be announced later.

item.title: Venue
item.description:
TNSAA 2026 will be held at:

RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS)

Kobe, Japan

The RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS) is one of the world’s leading research centers for high-performance computing and computational science. The workshop venue is located in Kobe, a major international port city in western Japan.

Access

From Kansai International Airport (KIX)

  • Airport Limousine Bus to Sannomiya Station
  • JR train connections available
  • Approx. 70–90 minutes

From Shin-Kobe Station

  • Subway + Port Liner access
  • Approx. 30 minutes

Local Transportation

Detailed transportation information will be provided closer to the workshop dates.
